title: Private keys stored as plaintext in SQLite
description: db.rs:14 stores private_key as TEXT NOT NULL in the personas table with no encryption at rest. persona.rs:36-43 inserts the raw PEM string directly. While server.rs:64-80 warns about file permissions, the private key is fully readable by anyone who can access the SQLite file (DB backups, log shipping, disk forensics). This is a confidentiality risk for key material. Mitigation is partial (file permission warning), but the schema should at minimum document this as a known plaintext storage decision, or ideally encrypt private keys at rest using a passphrase or KDF-derived key. Locations: db.rs:14 (schema), persona.rs:31-43 (insert), persona.rs:129-137 (read back as plain String).
close_reason: Accepted per threat model: 'Trust the database, filesystem, and operator.' Encrypting at rest requires passphrase/HSM, out of scope for v0.1.
